title: Kaworu died for your sins
wikitext:
[]: "Kaworu died for your sins"             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Sun Jan 30, 2011 9:49 pm I've now seen the "Kaworu died for your sins" bumper sticker made for Eva's 10th anniversary, and I'm just curious: where did the joke come from? Was it because Kaworu sacrificed himself to save humanity, or something else I'm not thinking of? (For the record, I'm a Christian, and not offended at all by that - I couldn't stop laughing once I saw that bumper sticker.)

[]:             Quote Post by esselfortium » Sun Jan 30, 2011 9:49 pm I believe you've pretty much got it, yes.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Sun Jan 30, 2011 10:07 pm Silly Packers fan, Optimus Prime died for your sins! (seriously though, first time I've heard of this. After a Google search, I am now interested that it was officially published by ADV; makes me wonder on how humorous Ganiax found it)

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun Jan 30, 2011 11:33 pm ^I was going to say that, but yeah

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Mon Jan 31, 2011 12:54 am More info related to that (and the boxset in general) here: http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1375

[]:             Quote Post by shikinami » Mon Jan 31, 2011 12:55 am LOL i want one!

[]:             Quote Post by DireDireDoodler » Mon Jan 31, 2011 3:07 am shikinami wrote: LOL i want one!

[]:             Quote Post by soul.assassin » Mon Jan 31, 2011 8:20 am

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 31, 2011 10:02 am I think someone should create an Eva Geeks branded version of this, and post it in suitable high resolution so that anyone can freely create their own sticker.

[]:             Quote Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Mon Jan 31, 2011 12:08 pm ^and there should also be a asterisk with Optimus Prime getting a mention in small print at the bottom

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Jan 31, 2011 1:12 pm THE Hal E. Burton 9000 wrote: ^and there should also be a asterisk with Optimus Prime getting a mention in small print at the bottom

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Mon Jan 31, 2011 5:58 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: No, Optimus Prime died because Hotrod had to go hotroddin around like a boob.

[]:             Quote Post by C.A.P. » Mon Jan 31, 2011 8:59 pm Transformers: Animated. I don't care for the franchise myself (Zero Punctuation nailed on why that is), but there you go.

[]:             Quote Post by child of Lilith » Mon Jan 31, 2011 11:32 pm soul.assassin wrote:

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Tue Feb 01, 2011 4:43 pm C.A.P. wrote: Transformers: Animated.

[]:             Quote Post by Grimmjow » Wed Feb 02, 2011 12:53 pm I'm also a Christian and I find it funny. It does kind of make sense if you consider Kaworu to be the Christ figure. Not to mention the fact that he's an Angel with the soul of Adam that takes a human body which is similar to God becoming manifested as a human. However, if you consider Kaworu to be the evil instrument used to destroy little ole Shinji's last grasp on sanity, well it's up for interpretation. :P

[]:             Quote Post by AshPhoenix » Wed Feb 02, 2011 3:53 pm Grimmjow wrote: It does kind of make sense if you consider Kaworu to be the Christ figure.

[]:             Quote Post by CyberXIII » Wed Feb 02, 2011 8:36 pm No, but he would die for it and leave the poor thing even more traumatized than before...

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Feb 02, 2011 9:00 pm AshPhoenix wrote: "Kind of"? It makes PERFECT sense.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Wed Feb 02, 2011 9:22 pm Rei is infinitely closer to Christ than Kaworu or Shinji (the latter is almost potently opposite of Jesus in EoE; what I liked to think of as corrective allusion). But Rei, I mean, she's both one with and part of the God that created humanity and her only purpose for existing as a "human" is to sacrifice herself so others can live. It's so funny because she's the one who selflessly sacrifices herself in EoE for someone else while Shinji gets literally crucified and becomes everyone's selfish executioner.

Was it because Kaworu sacrificed himself to save humanity, or something else I'm not thinking of? (For the record, I'm a Christian, and not offended at

[]: "Kaworu died for your sins"             Quote Post by Crazy Packers Fan » Sun Jan 30, 2011 9:49 pm I've now seen the "Kaworu died for your sins" bumper sticker made for Eva's 10th anniversary, and I'm just curious: where did the joke come from? Was it because Kaworu sacrificed himself to save humanity, or something else I'm not thinking of? (For the record, I'm a Christian, and not offended at all by that - I couldn't stop laughing once I saw that bumper sticker.)
